stories.title: Dowagiac residents get first look at data center plans Monday
stories.dek: The city council is hosting a public forum to discuss potential data center developments. Locals are concerned about noise, water use, and strain on the electric grid. The meeting gives residents a chance to voice questions before any formal proposals advance.
stories.source_title: Dowagiac City Council will hold data center forum Monday - WSBT

stories.title: Green Oak Township creates new zoning rules for power-hungry data centers
stories.dek: The board approved an overlay district to regulate facilities that demand high resources like electricity and water. This gives the township tools to control where large data centers can locate. Residents in Livingston County now have clearer guardrails on future projects.
stories.source_title: Green Oak Township Board Approves “High Resource Demand Facilities Overlay District” - WHMI

stories.title: Michigan town drags data center into federal court over nonstop noise
stories.dek: Residents claim the facility’s constant hum violates local quality of life and has gone unaddressed by regulators. The lawsuit seeks court intervention after local complaints produced no relief. A win could set precedent for other Michigan communities facing similar operations.
stories.source_title: AI data center's endless hum – the unchecked AI nightmare forcing a Michigan town into federal court - MSN

stories.title: Pere Marquette Township Board to Consider One-Year Data Center Pause
stories.dek: Trustees will review a proposed moratorium citing energy, water and noise impacts before any projects arrive, alongside a $1.36 million park entrance contract.

stories.title: Rural push kills Consumers 1.4 GW plant before it reaches grid
stories.dek: Residents in western Michigan blocked a major new gas plant that would have supplied power to data centers and other large users. The utility has now withdrawn the proposal entirely. Without the plant, ratepayers face tighter supply and possible higher costs as AI facilities keep demanding more electricity.
stories.source_title: Rural Michigan residents force Consumers Energy to drop 1.4GW power plant proposal - The Cool Down

stories.title: State lawmakers eye new guardrails for Michigan data centers
stories.dek: A legislative roundup shows bills that would set stricter rules on data-center power use and tax incentives. The measures would affect every township now fielding proposals from hyperscale developers. Passage would change what local boards can approve without state oversight.
stories.source_title: June 2026 legislative roundup: Mail-in-vote laws, tuition reimbursement tax credit and data center guardrails - The Michigan Daily

stories.title: Lansing hearing set on six-month data center pause after $120M project withdrawal
stories.dek: Council will consider the moratorium Monday to update zoning rules following resident backlash that killed a major proposed development.

stories.title: Consumers warns rates will rise if tax fight with state drags on
stories.dek: The utility says it is paying millions in disputed taxes that it may pass to customers. The dispute centers on how data-center projects are valued for property taxes. Any increase would hit households across the Lower Peninsula this winter.
stories.source_title: Consumers Energy warns of increased rates amid tax dispute with state - AOL.com

stories.title: Secret tax breaks for data centers already cost Michigan $13M
stories.dek: The incentives stay hidden from public view while draining state coffers. Local services lose funding that would otherwise come from these large facilities. Michigan taxpayers foot the bill without knowing which projects benefit.
stories.source_title: Data center tax breaks cost Michigan at least $13M but are shrouded in secrecy  - MLive.com

stories.title: Bay County freezes all new data center proposals
stories.dek: Bay County commissioners voted a temporary moratorium on data centers to study their impact on local power and water supplies. Residents could face higher utility bills if large facilities strain the grid during peak summer demand. The pause gives officials time to write rules before any projects advance.
stories.source_title: Bay County enacts temporary moratorium regarding data centers - AOL.com

stories.title: Garfield Twp planners back option to freeze new data centers
stories.dek: A township panel has advanced a moratorium proposal while residents debate the impact of large-scale facilities on local power and land use. The move comes as data-center interest grows in northwest Michigan. Grand Traverse County residents could see development paused if township leaders adopt the recommendation.
stories.source_title: Garfield Township panel recommends moratorium option amid data center debate - WPBN

national.title: Data centers advance rapidly as regulators scramble for rules
national.dek: Across the U.S., officials race to impose guardrails on power-hungry facilities while communities push back on incentives and impacts.

meetings.topic: Board of Trustees meeting, with data center-related items expected on agenda amid resident opposition
One-year moratorium on new data center development and $1.36M park project
Public hearing on temporary moratorium on new data center proposals
Extension of data center moratorium and related ordinances
Special meeting to consider Industrial Facilities Tax Exemption Certificate for Oracle's $43 billion data center project
